title: MBLEx D7 Guidelines for Professional Practice v1

questions.prompt: Which draping practice best supports client comfort and professional standards?

choices.A: Assume consent if the client didn’t object.
choices.B: Pause and obtain consent before changing techniques or pressure.
choices.C: Minimize draping to speed up the session.
choices.D: Avoid checking in so you don’t interrupt relaxation.

questions.answer: Pause and obtain consent before changing techniques or pressure.
questions.why: This option best follows standard-of-care principles while maintaining professional boundaries and client-centered communication.
questions.trap: Assume consent if the client didn’t object. is tempting, but it conflicts with professional standards.

questions.prompt: Which statement best reflects professional confidentiality practices?

choices.A: Set boundaries; if behavior continues, end the session and document.
choices.B: Agree and share your opinion about the colleague.
choices.C: Confirm client details publicly when replying online.
choices.D: Ignore inappropriate behavior to avoid conflict.

questions.answer: Set boundaries; if behavior continues, end the session and document.
questions.why: This option best follows standard-of-care principles while maintaining professional boundaries and client-centered communication.
questions.trap: Agree and share your opinion about the colleague. is tempting, but it conflicts with professional standards.

questions.prompt: Which documentation practice is most appropriate for maintaining a clear treatment record?

choices.A: Store records on a shared device without password protection.
choices.B: Use subjective labels like “dramatic” or “noncompliant.”
choices.C: Document thoroughly with relevant findings, but avoid adding personal opinions.
choices.D: Create a dated, clearly labeled late entry without altering the original note.

questions.answer: Create a dated, clearly labeled late entry without altering the original note.
questions.why: Among plausible options, this choice best satisfies professional standards while minimizing risk and maintaining appropriate boundaries.
questions.trap: Store records on a shared device without password protection. is tempting, but it conflicts with professional standards.

questions.prompt: Which practice best reduces risk of therapist overuse injury across a busy day?

choices.A: Lean from the low back instead of shifting weight.
choices.B: Lock your elbows to apply more pressure.
choices.C: Adjust table height so you can work with relaxed shoulders and neutral wrists.
choices.D: Skip breaks to maximize productivity.

questions.answer: Adjust table height so you can work with relaxed shoulders and neutral wrists.
questions.why: This option aligns with standard professional practice and supports client welfare, safety, and professionalism.
questions.trap: Lean from the low back instead of shifting weight. is tempting, but it conflicts with professional standards.

questions.prompt: Which communication practice best supports a professional therapeutic relationship?

choices.A: Extend the session for free to make up lost time.
choices.B: Let the client decide the end time each visit.
choices.C: Send medical advice by text to seem helpful.
choices.D: Begin promptly and explain that the session will end at the scheduled time.

questions.answer: Begin promptly and explain that the session will end at the scheduled time.
questions.why: This option best follows standard-of-care principles while maintaining professional boundaries and client-centered communication.
questions.trap: Extend the session for free to make up lost time. is tempting, but it conflicts with professional standards.

questions.prompt: Mid‑session you propose a technique change that may be uncomfortable. What is the best next step?

choices.A: Proceed because the technique is standard practice.
choices.B: Maintain appropriate draping and client privacy throughout.
choices.C: Assume consent if the client didn’t object.
choices.D: Minimize draping to speed up the session.

questions.answer: Maintain appropriate draping and client privacy throughout.
questions.why: This option best follows standard-of-care principles while maintaining professional boundaries and client-centered communication.
questions.trap: Proceed because the technique is standard practice. is tempting, but it conflicts with professional standards.

questions.prompt: A vendor offers you a bonus for recommending their product to clients. What is the most appropriate response?

choices.A: Recommend products only from vendors who pay you.
choices.B: Guarantee results to attract more clients.
choices.C: Accept large gifts to avoid offending clients.
choices.D: Provide transparent fees and document payments consistently.

questions.answer: Provide transparent fees and document payments consistently.
questions.why: This option aligns with standard professional practice and supports client welfare, safety, and professionalism.
questions.trap: Recommend products only from vendors who pay you. is tempting, but it conflicts with professional standards.

questions.prompt: A client messages you about health details via social media. What is the most appropriate response?

choices.A: Redirect to professional channels and protect confidentiality.
choices.B: Ignore inappropriate behavior to avoid conflict.
choices.C: Agree and share your opinion about the colleague.
choices.D: Discuss client cases with friends for advice.

questions.answer: Redirect to professional channels and protect confidentiality.
questions.why: This option best follows standard-of-care principles while maintaining professional boundaries and client-centered communication.
questions.trap: Ignore inappropriate behavior to avoid conflict. is tempting, but it conflicts with professional standards.
